_note: CONSTRUCTED evaluation scenario for H7 (defeasible reconciliation). Claims are illustrative simplifications of the cited tables. This directory is intentionally separate from seed_data/rules/ and is never seeded into, or counted toward, the verified rule corpus.
description: Specificity (lex specialis) within a single jurisdiction tier: the general maximum grade (5%) is relaxed by a terrain-conditional exception (8% in mountainous terrain). Both claims share jurisdiction, priority, and authority, so only the satisfied terrain condition separates them — the defeasible-exception pattern. A 6.5% grade is noncompliant on level terrain and compliant once terrain_type=mountainous is established.

claims.name: Maximum Grade - General (AASHTO, constructed simplification)
claims.citation: AASHTO Green Book, 7th Ed., Ch. 7, Table 7-2 (constructed simplification)
claims.description: General default: rural arterial grades at most 5%.

claims.name: Maximum Grade - Mountainous Exception (AASHTO, constructed simplification)
claims.citation: AASHTO Green Book, 7th Ed., Ch. 7, Table 7-2 (constructed simplification)
claims.description: Terrain exception: grades up to 8% permitted in mountainous terrain.
